response.justification: The candidate is an experienced, versatile software developer, but their background is predominantly web and full-stack (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang) rather than embedded firmware. Critical requirements are unmet: there is no evidence of native-level Japanese proficiency, no Linux kernel investigation or modification experience, and no exposure to L2/L3 networking, switch products, or security technologies like MACsec or 802.1X. C experience is limited to a brief stint at National Instruments a decade ago.
strengths: Broad, long-running software career spanning web, mobile, desktop, and some embedded systems
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, testing culture, and open-source contributions
Some C/C++ and low-latency systems experience (National Instruments, Dunnhumby/Sociomantic)
concerns: No native-level Japanese proficiency listed — a hard requirement for this role
No Linux kernel, embedded networking, switch, or security-protocol (MACsec, 802.1X) experience
C for UNIX experience is minimal and dated; recent work is web/full-stack focused

key_factors.factor: Solid general engineering experience and practices
